title: 2 Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
body_text:
2	Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
2.1	Definitions
CEBA
Canada Emergency Business Account
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
EDC
Export Development Canada
ServiceNow
ServiceNow is the web-based ticketing application used to report service issues, request changes to existing services, or submit questions to Central 1.
2.2	Frequently Asked Questions
General
Question 1
Once 75% or 66% of the loan is repaid, how do we process the forgivable 25% or 33% to close the loan?
Answer
When you report payments using the CEBA Repayment form in ServiceNow, you also report the date and amount of the 25% or 33% forgivable amount when you credit your customer. When 75% of $40,000.00 or 66% of $60,000.00 is repaid and 25% or 33% of the forgiven principal is reported, the loan process is complete. ServiceNow will not indicate that a loan is closed/completed, since your banking host is the book of record. If your customer wishes to confirm their account status with EDC, they can call the CEBA Call Centre at
1-888-324-4201
.
Question 2
Who is responsible in the event of fraudulent actions under the CEBA program?
Answer
The risk of fraud in relation to CEBA is allocated as set out in Section 3 and Schedule B of the Amended and Restated Program Administration Agreement. Please refer to the latest program documentation for further details. If you have any questions or concerns, please consult your legal counsel.
Reporting Requirements
Question 3
What is the reporting deadline for loan repayments?
Answer
Your financial institution submits two repayment reports to Central 1 every month.
Customer Makes Payment
FI reports to Central 1
Between 1st and 15th of month
By end of 22nd day of the same month
Between 16th and last day of month
By end of 7th day of the following month
Question 4
How do I report a previous payment made outside of the current reporting period?
Answer
The payment date (even for previous payments made outside of the current reporting period) should be reported as the current date within the current reporting period.
Question 5
What does the reporting process look like for loan administration fees?
Answer
Your financial institution is required to report the loan administration fee to Central 1 for CEBA loan applications. The fee must be reported at the end of each quarter before the 15th day after quarter end.
Question 6
I missed my CEBA admin fee reporting window for this quarter. Can I send it now/late?
Answer
No. If you miss the reporting window for a quarter (e.g., March 15), you can add the missed quarterâs admin fee amount into your next quarterâs reporting (June 15). You can only add in the previously missed quarter â not two or more previously missed quarters, if applicable. The fee floor only applies to the current quarter.
Question 7
How can I see previous loan administration fee payments?
Answer
There is a credit memo on your CAS account noting âCEBA Admin Fee for <<year and quarter>>â. ServiceNow forms wonât reflect a âPaidâ status after a quarterly submission. The forms only reflect the EDC submission date.
END
